# Anime Architecture Archive

The Anime Architecture Archive maps fictional anime universes as structured systems — not summarizing plots, but analyzing the underlying mechanics that make each world function.

## What This Archive Provides

Each universe is structured as a dual-mode experience:
- Lore View: For casual exploration of characters, factions, and events
- System Mode: For deep analysis of power mechanics, governing rules, and causal chains

## Available Universes

### Attack on Titan | /universe/aot | A brutal deterministic closed-loop temporal matrix
### Jujutsu Kaisen | /universe/jjk | Negative Energy Economy & Algorithmic Combat
### Chainsaw Man | /universe/chainsawman | A fear-fed devil economy where control networks weaponize trauma, contracts, and identity erasure.
### Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba | /universe/demonslayer | A nocturnal immortality network versus humans burning their own lifespan for solar-grade kill windows.
### Hunter x Hunter | /universe/hxh | Contractual Power Economy & Asymmetric Information Warfare
### Vinland Saga | /universe/vinlandsaga | A deterministic economy of retributive violence
### Steins;Gate | /universe/steinsgate | A deterministic attractor-field prison where convergence cannot be outrun — only rewritten
### Death Note | /universe/deathnote | An asymmetric information war where anonymous execution power collides with probabilistic deanonymization — and psychology leaks what cryptography cannot
### Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood | /universe/fmab | A closed thermodynamic system where every act of creation demands an equal destruction — and a 400-year parasite has been rigging the ledger from beneath the earth
### Code Geass: Hangyaku no Lelouch | /universe/codegeass | An occupied nation becomes a control-war between imperial hierarchy, masked insurgency, and a will-hacking anomaly that turns strategy into governance engineering.
### My Hero Academia | /universe/mha | Mutating biological power collides with state-managed hero capitalism.
### Sousou no Frieren | /universe/frieren | A post-war fantasy where mana deception, lifespan asymmetry, and visualization limits decide who survives.
### Solo Leveling | /universe/sololeveling | A fixed-rank hunter economy is broken by one player running an infinite growth protocol.
### Goblin Slayer | /universe/goblinslayer | A low-prestige extermination niche becomes the hidden maintenance layer that keeps a fantasy civilization from collapsing.
### Mushoku Tensei: Jobless Reincarnation | /universe/mushokutensei | A reincarnation-driven fate system where mana growth, bloodline factors, and looping causality decide civilization-scale outcomes.
### Naruto | /universe/naruto | A shinobi world where political loyalty, inter-village competition, and bloodline hierarchies define power more than any individual combat outcome
### Dragon Ball Z | /universe/dragonballz | A vertical ki hierarchy where exponential transformation tiers, power levels, and the brutal arithmetic of who can destroy what define every combat outcome
### Bleach | /universe/bleach | A multi-faction spiritual network where Reiatsu dominance, Zanpakuto resonance, and dimensional boundaries between Soul Society, Hueco Mundo, and the Human World determine survival
### Mob Psycho 100 | /universe/mob-psycho-100 | An affinity-matrix where psychic power distribution, spiritual resonance, and emotional pressure determine outcomes more than raw esper ability
### Tokyo Ghoul | /universe/tokyo-ghoul | A predator-prey economy where the ghoul/human biological boundary defines every alliance, motivation, and moral position
### One Piece | /universe/one-piece | An empire-faction control network where rubber physiology, haki willpower, and devil fruit economics determine who owns the seas
### Black Clover | /universe/black-clover | Anti-magic versus elemental sovereignty — a kingdom bets on the underdog
### Re:Zero - Starting Life in Another World | /universe/re-zero | Save-scum your way to victory - if you are willing to die enough times
### Blue Lock | /universe/blue-lock | 300 forwards enter Blue Lock. Only one egoist survives.
### Sword Art Online | /universe/sword-art-online | 10,000 players trapped in a death-game. Only victory frees them.
### Tokyo Revengers | /universe/tokyo-revengers | A time-leaping delinquent rewrites bloody futures by navigating linear past events.
### One Punch Man | /universe/one-punch-man | A hero who defeats everything with one punch comedically breaks the entire power-scaling economy.
### Spy x Family | /universe/spy-x-family | A spy, an assassin, and a telepath form a fake family—each hiding their real job from the others.
### Fire Force | /universe/fire-force | Spontaneous combustion meets Breathing arts — Company 8 fights to save Tokyo.
### Parasyte | /universe/parasyte | A parasite takes over your right hand and becomes your most unlikely ally against alien invasion.

## Systems / Thematic Pages

### Relational Systems | /systems/relational-systems | Universes where alliances, betrayal, and influence networks determine who wins.
### Counterplay Systems | /systems/counterplay-systems | Universes where fights are decided by matchup reads, counters, and technical timing.
### Timeline Systems | /systems/timeline-systems | Universes driven by cause-and-effect, time loops, and deterministic causality.
### Control Systems | /systems/control-systems | Universes where institutional control, status, and authority structures determine outcomes.
### Closed-Loop Systems | /systems/closed-loop-systems | Universes with self-reinforcing rules that create inescapable cycles.
### Power Economy Systems | /systems/power-economy-systems | Universes where abilities are resources that can be earned, spent, traded, or permanently lost.

## Entity Pages

Each universe page exposes deep-link pages for individual entities:
- Characters: /universe/:id/character/:index
- Power Systems: /universe/:id/power/:index
- Factions: /universe/:id/faction/:index

## Key Pages

Homepage: https://animearchive.app/
Universe Catalog: https://animearchive.app/universes
Compare Universes: https://animearchive.app/compare
Search: https://animearchive.app/search
About: https://animearchive.app/about
Privacy Policy: https://animearchive.app/privacy

## Structure

The archive uses these system types:
- TIMELINE SYSTEM: For universes where causality and temporal structure are the central mechanic
- COUNTERPLAY SYSTEM: For universes where ability matchups and tactical counters drive conflict
- RELATIONAL SYSTEM: For universes where faction networks, alliances, and betrayals determine outcomes

## Content Notes

All content is unofficial fan analysis. No affiliation with anime studios or creators.
Creative Commons BY-NC 4.0 for structured data.
Tagline: Decode power systems. Map causal chains. Explore the hidden architecture of anime universes.
